soap:
RE-REVIEW · lancedb/lancedb#2829 — feat: support embedding at search on remote table
reviewed 655a02c3 → e7d6a857  (delta range spans 201 commits / 3 author-touched files; true PR net diff is 5 commits, +109/−32, 3 files)

CONDITIONS LEDGER (0/0 met)
  (no closed-ended conditions) — my prior review at 655a02c3 was a single COMMENTED review asking a question ("I'm surprised this wouldn't work already… why does this work now?"), with no posted issue(blocking) comments. The packet reconstructed 0 conditions; confirmed against the PR's review + inline-comment history.

FRESH-EYES DELTA

  ⚠ DELTA CONTAMINATION (read this first):
  The commit-anchored delta 655a02c3...e7d6a857 reports +671 lines across the 3 files. None of that is this PR's work. The base branch main advanced far past 655a02c3 and was merged into the PR branch (packet excluded 224 wholly-off-branch files; the 3 files surviving the scope filter are ones the PR also touches). PR is mergeable_state: dirty. Ground truth = GitHub net diff (.diff): +109/−32.

  The PR's actual change:
  - remote/table.py::search() — when query is a FullTextQuery set query_type="fts", then resolve the column via infer_vector_column_name(schema=self.schema, query_type, query, vector_column_name) before building the query. self.schema is a @property returning pa.Schema (verified), so the call signature is correct.
  - table.py — import reordering (isort) + the same search() whitespace tweak; the infer_vector_column_name call there already existed. No behavioral change.
  - test_remote_db.py — adds a schema fixture, a vector_column assertion to existing query tests, and test_query_sync_embedding_function_auto_vector covering the new path (embedding-function table, .search("puppy") with no explicit column → asserts body["vector_column"]=="vector" and the query embedding is computed).

  This answers my prior question: previously remote search() forwarded vector_column_name (often None) straight to the server with no client-side inference; now infer_vector_column_name does it client-side. Logic verified against the util.py definition at head — FTS → None, explicit name → passthrough, otherwise infer (clear ValueError on 0 or >1 vector columns).

  - issue (non-blocking): behavioral change for vector search on a table with no vector column. The new path calls inf_vector_column_query, which now raises ValueError client-side before the request is sent, whereas the old code forwarded None and let the server respond. Likely the intended improvement, but worth a one-line confirmation that no remote flow relied on the server handling a null column.

  No new bad code introduced by the author's actual diff. Test coverage for the new path is present and targeted.

RECOMMENDATION: approve (with one non-blocking note)
  confidence: medium-high on the author's real diff (small, well-tested, answers the open question). LOWER confidence that nothing harmful rides in via the merged-in main churn — but that churn is out of this PR's scope and should be re-reviewed at its own source. The dirty merge state must be resolved before merge.
  what would make this wrong: if RemoteTable.schema (the @property) triggers a network/describe call that fails or is expensive on every search() and that regresses callers — worth a glance, but the existing tests stub it and pass.
